$comment: Ready-to-create cloud routine for the weekly observatory run. Blocked on: (1) connect GitHub at claude.ai settings, (2) install the Claude GitHub App on sass-maker/fleet-workspace (https://claude.ai/code/onboarding?magic=github-app-setup). Then any Claude Code session can create it via the /schedule skill using this body (generate a fresh events[].data.uuid). Cron 30 2 * * 1 = Monday 08:00 IST.
name: GEO Observatory — weekly run

message.content:
Run the fleet GEO Observatory weekly measurement.

You are in a checkout of sass-maker/fleet-workspace. Read fleet-ops/skills/geo-observatory/SKILL.md and follow its protocol exactly:

1. Load fleet-ops/config/geo-observatory.json. For every product query, run live WebSearch and classify A/B/C per the rubric in the skill (A = product's own origin in top 3 organic results; B = reachable on page 1 only via sassmaker.com/GitHub/directories; C = absent from page 1). Capture top 2-3 evidence URLs and a one-line note per query.
2. Write all observations to a temp JSON file (fields: date = today YYYY-MM-DD, product, qid, class, top, notes) and run: node fleet-ops/scripts/geo-observatory-record.mjs <file>
3. If the script rejects entries, fix them and re-run; never edit the ledger or report by hand.
4. Commit fleet-ops/data/geo-observatory and fleet-ops/docs/geo-observatory-latest.md with message 'geo-observatory: <date> run (<n> observations)' and push to main.
5. End your session with a short summary: the Movers section verbatim, plus flags where a class contradicts shipped work (likely causes: not deployed / not indexed / robots-blocked).

Rules: never rephrase configured queries; evidence URLs required for every classification; a one-run class change is noted as tentative, not announced as a win; do not scrape AI-engine UIs; keep it to one focused pass — no scope expansion.
